title: Two Courts, One Company - TCR 04/09/26
description:
Two federal courts reached opposite legal conclusions about Anthropic in the same week - one calling the Pentagon's supply-chain risk designation "Orwellian," the other deferring to military judgment - leaving the question of whether embedded AI values constitute protected speech in genuine constitutional suspension until oral arguments on May 19.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-9-2026/

The D.C. Circuit refused to pause the Pentagon's supply-chain risk designation against Anthropic, directly contradicting a San Francisco judge who called the same designation unlawful and ordered it removed - creating unprecedented legal limbo where two federal courts hold opposite positions on the same government action against the same company. Meta Superintelligence Labs released Muse Spark, its first model from a $14.3 billion ground-up rebuild, leading all competitors on medical reasoning while trailing on abstract reasoning and breaking from Meta's open-source heritage by launching as closed-source. Anthropic simultaneously shipped Claude Managed Agents, enterprise infrastructure for deploying autonomous agent fleets that shifts the bottleneck from "can we build it" to "what should it do." Fervo Energy signed a 1.75-gigawatt turbine supply agreement addressing the last major bottleneck for enhanced geothermal at scale. New Jersey became the sixth state to repeal its nuclear construction moratorium. The UK approved an 800-megawatt solar farm, Norway ordered 20 electric hydrofoil ferries, and CISA warned that Iranian-affiliated hackers are actively disrupting programmable logic controllers across U.S. critical infrastructure.

Read the full edition: https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-9-2026/
